description: iOS SDK — FirebaseStorage class, BackupMethods.Firebase enum, X-Firebase-Token header, backup/recovery/eject flows, example app updates

tasks.task: Create feature branch and explore codebase
tasks.description: 1) cd into repo, 2) git checkout -b rami/eng-7411-firebase-auth-swift-changes, 3) Explore the project structure to find: BackupMethods enum, PortalStorage protocol/interface, existing backup method implementations (Password, Passkey, iCloud, GDrive), how the Portal class registers and uses backup methods, the HTTP client that talks to TBS, the existing example app(s). Map out the key files that need modification.

tasks.task: Add Firebase case to BackupMethods enum
tasks.description: Find the BackupMethods enum (likely in a types or models file). Add case Firebase = 'FIREBASE' following the pattern of existing cases (GoogleDrive, iCloud, local, Password, Passkey). Ensure it's Codable and works with existing serialization.

tasks.task: Create FirebaseStorage class conforming to PortalStorage protocol
tasks.description: Create a new class FirebaseStorage that conforms to the PortalStorage protocol (or whatever the storage interface is called). Constructor takes a getToken callback: () async throws -> String? that returns a fresh Firebase ID token. The class stores the callback and calls it when performing backup/recovery/eject operations. Look at existing storage implementations (PasswordStorage, PasskeyStorage, ICloudStorage, GDriveStorage) for the exact protocol conformance required.

tasks.task: Add X-Firebase-Token header to TBS requests for Firebase backup
tasks.description: Modify the HTTP client or request builder so that when backup method is Firebase, it includes X-Firebase-Token header with the token from getToken() callback. The Authorization header still carries the client API key. Find where TBS requests are constructed (likely in a networking/API layer) and add support for this additional header.

tasks.task: Implement Firebase backup flow (store encryption key)
tasks.description: When portal.backupWallet(method: .Firebase) is called: 1) Call getToken() to get Firebase ID token, 2) Generate or use existing encryption key, 3) PUT /v1/backup/encrypt-key with Authorization: Bearer {client_api_key} + X-Firebase-Token: {firebase_id_token} headers and {encryptionKey} body, 4) Store backup share to customer storage. Skip WebAuthn ceremony entirely. Follow the pattern of existing backup implementations but route through the Firebase endpoints instead of passkey endpoints.

tasks.task: Implement Firebase recovery flow (retrieve encryption key)
tasks.description: When portal.recoverWallet(method: .Firebase) is called: 1) Call getToken() to get Firebase ID token, 2) GET /v1/backup/encrypt-key with Authorization + X-Firebase-Token headers, 3) Use returned encryptionKey to decrypt backup share from customer storage, 4) Run re-share protocol with Portal's backup share, 5) Generate new signing shares. Follow existing recovery flow patterns.

When portal.eject(method: .Firebase) is called: follow the eject flow but use Firebase auth — GET encryption key via Firebase endpoint (GET /v1/backup/encrypt-key with X-Firebase-Token), then proceed with standard eject logic using the retrieved key. Look at existing eject implementation for the pattern.

tasks.task: Add token refresh and 401 retry logic
tasks.description: Handle 401 responses from TBS Firebase endpoints by calling getToken() again (force refresh) and retrying the request once. Firebase ID tokens expire after 1 hour. Only retry once to avoid loops. Apply to both PUT and GET encrypt-key requests.

Ensure portal.registerBackupMethod(.Firebase, withStorage: FirebaseStorage(...)) works. The Portal class should store the FirebaseStorage instance and use it when backup/recover/eject is called with method: .Firebase. Check how existing backup methods are registered and follow the same pattern.

tasks.task: Update example app to demonstrate Firebase backup
tasks.description: Find the existing example/demo app in the repo. Add Firebase backup demonstration: 1) Add Firebase SDK dependency (FirebaseAuth) to the example app, 2) Add a 'Backup with Firebase' button/flow, 3) Show how to register FirebaseStorage with a getToken callback using FirebaseAuth.auth().currentUser?.getIDToken(), 4) Demonstrate backup, recovery, and eject via Firebase. Add comments explaining each step. The example should be a working reference for customers.

tasks.task: Write unit tests for Firebase backup/recovery/eject
tasks.description: Test: backup stores key correctly (mock TBS response), recovery retrieves key correctly, eject works, token refresh on 401 (mock 401 then 200), missing token callback error, error handling for TBS errors (500, 403, 404), concurrent backup methods (passkey + Firebase coexist). Use XCTest framework and mock the networking layer.
Build the project (xcodebuild or swift build). Run tests. Fix any compiler warnings or errors. Commit all changes to branch rami/eng-7411-firebase-auth-swift-changes. Do NOT push to main.
